definition_en: A list of topics to be discussed at a meeting
example: Please review the agenda before the meeting.

definition_en: A computer program used to organise and calculate data in rows and columns
example: She created a spreadsheet to track the monthly expenses.

definition_en: An official written record of what was discussed at a meeting
example: Could you take the minutes at today's meeting?

definition_en: A telephone or video call involving three or more people
example: We have a conference call with the New York team at 3 pm.

definition_en: A small enclosed area for one person to work in within an office
example: She personalised her cubicle with photos and plants.

definition_en: A short written note sent within an organisation
example: The director sent a memo about the new policy.

definition_en: Writing and office materials such as pens, paper and envelopes
example: We need to order more stationery for the office.

definition_en: The latest time by which something must be completed
example: The deadline for the project is next Monday.

definition_en: The place where incoming emails or letters are received
example: I have over 100 unread messages in my inbox.

definition_en: A piece of furniture with drawers for storing documents
example: The contracts are in the filing cabinet in the corner.

definition_en: To generate a lot of ideas in a group session without judging them
example: Let's brainstorm ideas for the new marketing campaign.

definition_en: A formal talk or demonstration to an audience
example: She gave a presentation on the quarterly results.

definition_en: A working space with few or no dividing walls
example: The open-plan office encourages collaboration.

definition_en: The system of levels of authority in an organisation
example: The company has a flat hierarchy with few management levels.

definition_en: A copy of a document made by a machine
example: Can you photocopy these five pages for me?

definition_en: To give formal approval for something
example: The manager needs to sign off the budget before we proceed.

definition_en: A pre-designed document used as a starting point for creating other documents
example: Use the company template for all official letters.

definition_en: To take further action after an initial contact or task
example: I'll follow up with the client after the meeting.

definition_en: To give a task or responsibility to someone else
example: Good managers know how to delegate effectively.

definition_en: Key Performance Indicator — a measure used to evaluate success
example: Sales conversion rate is one of our main KPIs.

definition_en: The practice of sharing desks in an office instead of having assigned seats
example: Hot-desking saves space but some employees find it disruptive.

definition_en: A trial period at the start of employment
example: She is still in her probation period for the first three months.
